title: Testimony

jokes.text: I would never, ever let that happen. And if it had happened, which it absolutely did not, I would make sure that something was done about it.
jokes.setup: Reporter asks about lobbying in her name
jokes.punchline: Selina's contradictory denial that simultaneously admits and denies wrongdoing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina character moment - the logic pretzel of denying something while detailing what she'd do if it happened shows her inability to stick to a simple denial

jokes.text: Don't touch me.
jokes.setup: Press conference ending pleasantly
jokes.punchline: Selina's harsh rejection of normal human contact
jokes.explanation: Brutally efficient character beat - three words that perfectly capture Selina's contempt for regular people

jokes.text: Rain gonna fall, bar's gonna close, bill's gonna fail.
jokes.setup: Asked why such an important bill failed
jokes.punchline: Treats massive political failure as inevitable as weather
jokes.explanation: Great rhythm and philosophical resignation - treats political catastrophe as natural law

jokes.text: There's a job for you at Hallmark, Mr. Cafferty.
jokes.setup: Cafferty's folksy wisdom about bills failing
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic suggestion he write greeting cards
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback that mocks Cafferty's attempt at homespun wisdom

jokes.text: If I had kids, which actually I do,
jokes.setup: Setting up hypothetical about having kids
jokes.punchline: Immediate correction that he does have kids
jokes.explanation: Perfect mid-sentence correction that reveals Cafferty's scattered thinking under pressure

jokes.text: this bill would be my baby. I and the entire White House, we sweated a full transfusion of blood to pass this bill.
jokes.setup: Explaining his commitment to the bill
jokes.punchline: Escalates from 'baby' metaphor to impossible medical imagery
jokes.explanation: Cafferty's tendency to overcomplicate metaphors - 'full transfusion of blood' is wonderfully overwrought

jokes.text: Read my luscious lips, no.
jokes.setup: Denying Jonah was sent to undermine the bill
jokes.punchline: Bizarre modification of 'read my lips' with 'luscious'
jokes.explanation: Cafferty's weird vanity showing through even in denial - 'luscious' is perfectly inappropriate for the context

jokes.text: You're not telling me the difference, you're just turning nouns into verbs.
jokes.setup: Witness tries to explain consultant vs lobbyist distinction
jokes.punchline: Committee member calls out the linguistic nonsense
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about political doublespeak - cuts through the verbal gymnastics perfectly

jokes.text: I'm sorry that I misconducted myself and I will not do that in to the future.
jokes.setup: Jonah trying to apologize professionally
jokes.punchline: Creates non-word 'misconducted' and adds unnecessary 'in'
jokes.explanation: Classic Jonah - trying to sound formal and smart but mangling basic grammar

jokes.text: Dan Egan is a solid five and a half, weak six.
jokes.setup: Asked to assess Dan Egan
jokes.punchline: Rates him like he's reviewing a restaurant
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah obliviousness - treats character assessment like a Yelp review, the precision of 'weak six' is hilarious

jokes.text: I mean, you could have Googled that.
jokes.setup: Committee asks about public record information
jokes.punchline: Jonah condescendingly tells Congressional committee to Google
jokes.explanation: Peak Jonah arrogance - lecturing a Congressional committee on basic research methods

jokes.text: we set fire to the wicker Dan.
jokes.setup: Explaining Dan being scapegoated
jokes.punchline: Turning 'wicker man' into 'wicker Dan'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels - pagan sacrifice imagery with casual Dan insertion

jokes.text: You make it sound like there's a correlation between what should happen and what actually happens.
jokes.setup: Asked why Dan deserved to go if innocent
jokes.punchline: Jonah accidentally delivers profound cynicism about justice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant accidental wisdom from Jonah - captures the absurdity of Washington while being completely unaware he's being profound

jokes.text: be that individual an actual person or a corporation.
jokes.setup: Amy talking about representing common citizens
jokes.punchline: Casually equates corporations with people
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire about Citizens United - treats corporate personhood as casual fact

jokes.text: That shoehorned stab at nobility will not fly here.
jokes.setup: Amy's patriotic speech about serving America
jokes.punchline: Committee member immediately calls out the BS
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Amy's attempt at noble spinning - 'shoehorned' perfectly describes the awkward patriotic pivot

jokes.text: I think it's interesting that you chose me to aim that question at, insinuating that I am a money-grabbing, careerist, low-moraled... I don't know why I'm helping you here.
jokes.setup: Amy defending herself against implications of greed
jokes.punchline: Realizes she's listing her actual negative traits
jokes.explanation: Perfect Amy self-awareness - catches herself mid-sentence accurately describing herself while trying to defend

jokes.text: So it's not a word combining Jonah and gonad?
jokes.setup: Witnesses deny knowledge of 'Jonad Files'
jokes.punchline: Committee member forces them to acknowledge the crude wordplay
jokes.explanation: Great setup for the nickname avalanche - forces acknowledgment of crude wordplay in formal setting

jokes.text: J-Rock, Jizzy Gillespie, Jack and the Giant Jackoff, Gaylien, Tinker Balls, Wadzilla, One Erection...
jokes.setup: Reading Jonah's nicknames to prove workplace hostility
jokes.punchline: Increasingly creative and crude nickname variations
jokes.explanation: Incredible creativity in juvenile humor - each nickname is a mini masterpiece of crude wordplay, 'Jizzy Gillespie' is particularly inspired

jokes.text: The Pointless Giant, The 60-Foot Virgin, Gimpanzee, Jonah Ono, Hagrid's Nutsack, Scrotum Pole, Transgenderformers, 12 Years a Slave to Jerking Off, Benedict Come In His Own Hand, Guyscraper, The Cloud Botherer, Supercalifragilisticexpiali Dick Cheese, Teenage Mutant Ninja Asshole, Spewbacca.
jokes.setup: Continuation of nickname reading
jokes.punchline: Escalating creativity and cultural reference mashups
jokes.explanation: Legendary moment - 'Supercalifragilisticexpiali Dick Cheese' and 'Benedict Come In His Own Hand' are comedic masterpieces. The escalation and cultural reference density is unprecedented

jokes.text: My college friends called me Tall McCartney. I preferred that. That's a good nickname.
jokes.setup: After hearing dozens of cruel nicknames about him
jokes.punchline: Jonah fondly remembers one nice nickname
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah response - completely misses the point of the exercise, nostalgic about a genuinely sweet nickname amid the cruelty

jokes.text: The drama queen in me likes to say that they sacrificed me at Easter.
jokes.setup: Asked about being fired from the White House
jokes.punchline: Compares firing to Christ's crucifixion
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware martyrdom complex - Lee acknowledging her tendency toward dramatic self-pity

jokes.text: No, I value confidentiality and paradoxically I don't care who knows it.
jokes.setup: Asked if she was responsible for data breach
jokes.punchline: Claims to value confidentiality while publicly announcing it
jokes.explanation: Clever self-contradictory logic - the paradox is genuinely witty and perfectly delivered

jokes.text: They thought I was a mouse. Well, this mouse will roar.
jokes.setup: Explaining why she was scapegoated
jokes.punchline: Mixing metaphors with mouse/lion imagery
jokes.explanation: Decent mixed metaphor comedy - Lee trying to sound tough while acknowledging she was seen as weak

jokes.text: Rat and roll. Deal?
jokes.setup: Dan offering to give information in exchange for immunity
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'rock and roll' for ratting out colleagues
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dan sleaziness - making betrayal sound cool with a catchy pun

jokes.text: Your judgment, ma'am, is unimpeachable.
jokes.setup: Suggestion that congresswoman's friendship with president clouds judgment
jokes.punchline: Lee's obvious, sarcastic flattery
jokes.explanation: Obvious suck-up moment that everyone can see through

jokes.text: she was so junior, she may have well been working in the kitchens, with all due respect to the kitchens.
jokes.setup: Bill dismissing Lee Patterson's knowledge
jokes.punchline: Backhanded apology to kitchen staff
jokes.explanation: Good Bill character moment - his pseudo-politeness while being dismissive

jokes.text: what Jimmy Cagney would have called a good kid in my opinion.
jokes.setup: Bill trying to soften his criticism of Lee
jokes.punchline: Random Jimmy Cagney reference in formal hearing
jokes.explanation: Weird Bill non-sequitur - the Jimmy Cagney reference is completely out of left field

jokes.text: This is what I was afraid of. I'm being snowballed. I'm a victim of snowballing.
jokes.setup: Bill confronted with evidence against him
jokes.punchline: Claims to be victim of 'snowballing'
jokes.explanation: Bill's panic manifesting as new victimization narrative - the repetition shows his desperation

jokes.text: a name gets mentioned, then gets mentioned again, and pretty soon everyone is mentioning it and then the whole thing snowballs and then there's an avalanche of snowballs and I'm being buried by other people's snow and it's not pure driven, it's not white snow, if you catch my drift.
jokes.setup: Bill trying to explain 'snowballing'
jokes.punchline: Metaphor spirals completely out of control with racial undertones
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bill meltdown - metaphor becomes increasingly incomprehensible and accidentally inappropriate

jokes.text: No, that was scapegoating. Again, apologies. You haven't asked me a question.
jokes.setup: Bill correcting his previous answer
jokes.punchline: Realizes he's answering unasked questions
jokes.explanation: Good Bill nervous energy - his overthinking leading to premature answers

jokes.text: It's not my fault. That's the difference.
jokes.setup: Asked difference between scapegoating and snowballing
jokes.punchline: Reduces complex concepts to simple self-defense
jokes.explanation: Brutally honest reduction of Bill's entire worldview to self-preservation

jokes.text: Oh, that's... bravo, Bill. Subtly done.
jokes.setup: Bill pointing finger at Kent as campaign manager
jokes.punchline: Kent's sarcastic praise for Bill's obvious deflection
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kent dryness - acknowledging Bill's transparent attempt to shift blame

jokes.text: Like a swan or Professor Hawking.
jokes.setup: Explaining there was lots going on underneath the surface
jokes.punchline: Compares political maneuvering to swan and Stephen Hawking
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Kent non-sequitur - the Hawking comparison is simultaneously inappropriate and oddly apt for hidden complexity

jokes.text: You haven't seen it, so forget it.
jokes.setup: Kent trying to make Usual Suspects reference
jokes.punchline: Abandons explanation when committee member hasn't seen it
jokes.explanation: Good Kent moment - his inability to adapt when his cultural reference fails

jokes.text: He's the bagman, yeah, but he's also the bad man.
jokes.setup: Describing Gary Walsh's role
jokes.punchline: Pun on bagman/bad man
jokes.explanation: Decent Kent wordplay - simple but effective pun that sounds like campaign slogan

jokes.text: it was a smaller hearing and executive privileges were waived and now it's like a U2 concert.
jokes.setup: Gary explaining why he's uncomfortable
jokes.punchline: Compares Congressional hearing to rock concert
jokes.explanation: Good Gary anxiety metaphor - the scale jump from small hearing to U2 concert captures his panic

jokes.text: That's a fault of mine... Just kind of believing that people were friends and they're not friends. It's kind of been going on since middle school and you just caught me at a bad time.
jokes.setup: Gary correcting himself about being friends with Dan
jokes.punchline: Reveals deep personal insecurity about friendship
jokes.explanation: Heartbreaking Gary moment - his fundamental loneliness exposed in formal setting

jokes.text: Uh, it's very intimate. I've always imagined myself as like a pipeline who carries a lot of necessary information and I just feed it directly into her head.
jokes.setup: Asked about relationship with president
jokes.punchline: Describes weirdly intimate 'pipeline' metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary creepiness - the 'pipeline' metaphor with 'feed it directly into her head' is wonderfully disturbing

jokes.text: Yes, and I have just then.
jokes.setup: Committee says she promised to help in any way
jokes.punchline: Claims saying 'no' to their question was helping
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina logic - technically correct but completely unhelpful, delivered with presidential authority

jokes.text: I'm a nobody. That's the truth. I'm a nobody.
jokes.setup: Asked about lobbying knowledge
jokes.punchline: Gary's desperate self-deprecation
jokes.explanation: Gary's pathetic defense strategy - claiming powerlessness to avoid responsibility

jokes.text: I'm not a powerful, guilty, arrogant man. I'm an arrogant man.
jokes.setup: Gary defending himself against implications
jokes.punchline: Admits to arrogance while denying other qualities
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Gary self-awareness - admits the one negative trait while denying the important ones

jokes.text: Um, Gary has a very limited set of skills. Mainly I would say they are picking objects up and then putting objects back down.
jokes.setup: Asked about Gary's competence
jokes.punchline: Reduces Gary to basic motor functions
jokes.explanation: Brutal Selina dismissal of Gary - reduces years of loyalty to object manipulation

jokes.text: I don't see that you're wearing a ring, so...
jokes.setup: Committee member asking about Catherine's personal life
jokes.punchline: Catherine attacks committee member's marital status
jokes.explanation: Good Catherine snark - turns personal questioning back on questioner

jokes.text: I'm sorry about that. I just had to get a snack. My blood sugar was dropping, so...
jokes.setup: Selina returning from break
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions snacking during serious hearing
jokes.explanation: Good Selina self-care prioritization - treating formal hearing like inconvenience

jokes.text: I have to go. I've got to call the president of Africa. So... I mean South Africa specifically, you know, so...
jokes.setup: Selina trying to end the questioning
jokes.punchline: Says 'president of Africa' then corrects to South Africa
jokes.explanation: Classic Selina geographic confusion - the correction doesn't really help her case

jokes.text: And, unlike in reality, I would have gotten Iwo Jima right.
jokes.setup: Sue being complimented on her organizational skills
jokes.punchline: Implies she could have improved WWII battle outcome
jokes.explanation: Perfect Sue arrogance - casually claiming she could have improved one of the bloodiest WWII battles

jokes.text: I mean, I once met Bill Gates, but I didn't have a 'meeting' with him.
jokes.setup: Explaining difference between meeting and having a meeting
jokes.punchline: Uses Bill Gates as example
jokes.explanation: Sue's attempt at relatable example - the Bill Gates namedrop is perfectly Sue

jokes.text: None of which you seem to be able to master at the present moment.
jokes.setup: Sue listing all their communication methods
jokes.punchline: Committee member points out she can't communicate clearly
jokes.explanation: Good burn on Sue's communication failures despite listing all their methods

jokes.text: Uh, I was getting some medicine. What kind of medicine? Um, it's knee medicine.
jokes.setup: Mike asked what he was getting from his car
jokes.punchline: Vague, suspicious answer about 'knee medicine'
jokes.explanation: Mike's terrible lying begins - 'knee medicine' is suspiciously vague

jokes.text: Crouch Cream. But that's not right. It's a white tube with a red label. I'm sure an intern could research this. It has a silhouette of a horse on it. I don't know why the horse is on there. I guess horses probably have good knees.
jokes.setup: Mike trying to name the knee medicine
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous details about fictional cream
jokes.explanation: Peak Mike panic - 'Crouch Cream' and horse knee logic is comedy gold. The escalation from simple lie to elaborate fiction is perfect

jokes.text: Knee-Z. Knee-Z Cream. I don't think that's it. It's searchable. I'm very positive.
jokes.setup: Mike still trying to name the medicine
jokes.punchline: Creates fake product name then doubts himself
jokes.explanation: Continuation of Mike's spiral - even doubts his own made-up product name

jokes.text: Um, I had grown quite fond of dear Jason.
jokes.setup: Selina acting upset about Catherine's breakup
jokes.punchline: Fake sentimentality after clearly orchestrating the split
jokes.explanation: Selina's transparent fake emotion - 'dear Jason' after calling him controlling

jokes.text: This may be a good day for truth, but a sad day for love.
jokes.setup: Selina's prepared statement about Catherine's breakup
jokes.punchline: Grandiose, fake sentiment about orchestrated breakup
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina grandiosity - turning personal manipulation into poetic statement

jokes.text: you want to give me your number? Or I'll just send it to myself.
jokes.setup: Mike demonstrating the app to committee
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly asks for committee member's number
jokes.explanation: Mike's social obliviousness - treating formal hearing like casual tech demo

jokes.text: I guess that's why they say don't work with animals or technology. I mean, that's why 'Jaws' was such a nightmare shoot. I mean, mechanical shark, forget it.
jokes.setup: Mike's app demonstration failing
jokes.punchline: Random Jaws reference to explain tech failure
jokes.explanation: Peak Mike randomness - connecting app failure to Jaws production problems

jokes.text: Oh, Knee Free. That's the cream. Knee Free, if you...
jokes.setup: Mike suddenly remembering the 'real' name
jokes.punchline: Another fake medicine name
jokes.explanation: Mike still can't let the knee medicine lie go - 'Knee Free' is third attempt

jokes.text: Probably about them... you know, mating.
jokes.setup: Trying to explain what Selina meant about Dan and Amy
jokes.punchline: Uses 'mating' like they're animals
jokes.explanation: Mike's awkward word choice - 'mating' in formal context is perfectly uncomfortable

jokes.text: I mean, why are you throwing pebbles at a tank?
jokes.setup: Mike defending Selina's competence
jokes.punchline: Random military metaphor for questioning the president
jokes.explanation: Another Mike random metaphor - treating Congressional oversight as futile attack

jokes.text: You know, that lady, she could govern herself through leprosy.
jokes.setup: Mike defending Selina's governing ability
jokes.punchline: Bizarre leprosy comparison
jokes.explanation: Peak Mike weirdness - leprosy as ultimate test of governing ability is wonderfully bizarre

jokes.text: I don't have time for friends.
jokes.setup: Asked about friendship with committee member
jokes.punchline: Selina's cold dismissal of human connection
jokes.explanation: Pure Selina efficiency - reduces human relationships to time management issue

jokes.text: I was touched. Who touched you? I would rather not say at this time. Or any.
jokes.setup: Jonah claiming emotional issues from being touched
jokes.punchline: Awkward sexual harassment revelation
jokes.explanation: Setup for extended Jonah molestation storyline - uncomfortable but character-appropriate

jokes.text: he touched me in a private place.
jokes.setup: Committee asking for specifics about touching
jokes.punchline: Jonah's euphemistic description
jokes.explanation: Jonah's childish language for adult situation - sets up more specific questioning

jokes.text: It was... it was around the other side.
jokes.setup: Asked if it was the buttocks
jokes.punchline: Jonah's awkward way of saying front/crotch
jokes.explanation: Jonah's inability to use direct language even when describing assault

jokes.text: Similarly with Ms. Brookheimer's dress. She can't have believed that this would work. She never looks this humble.
jokes.setup: Jonah complaining about committee being played
jokes.punchline: Calls out Amy's fake humble dress choice
jokes.explanation: Rare Jonah insight - correctly identifies Amy's strategic wardrobe choice

jokes.text: Like an Xbox.
jokes.setup: Jonah saying Amy is playing them 'like a cheap...'
jokes.punchline: Substitutes 'Xbox' for expected vulgar comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah self-censorship - Xbox is wonderfully inappropriate replacement for crude analogy

jokes.text: Mr. Ryan, trust me, no one is playing us. We are unplayable.
jokes.setup: Jonah claiming they're being manipulated
jokes.punchline: Committee member's confident assertion of immunity
jokes.explanation: 'Unplayable' is great Congressional arrogance - ironically proves Jonah's point

jokes.text: My... spheres were always clothed.
jokes.setup: Asked about testicle contact
jokes.punchline: Jonah's bizarre euphemism for testicles
jokes.explanation: Legendary Jonah euphemism - 'spheres' is hilariously formal for testicles

jokes.text: I can't stop thinking about it. It's... you know, it's like a bell. It can't be unrung.
jokes.setup: Dan describing witnessing Jonah's assault
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate bell metaphor for traumatic memory
jokes.explanation: Dan's weird processing of trauma - bell metaphor is oddly poetic for disturbing memory

jokes.text: It was around the spheres.
jokes.setup: Asked where the touching happened
jokes.punchline: Dan adopts Jonah's 'spheres' euphemism
jokes.explanation: Great callback - Dan unconsciously adopting Jonah's ridiculous euphemism

jokes.text: Gary Walsh, you need to understand, is a 12-year-old boy trapped in the body of a 12-year-old girl.
jokes.setup: Asked about Gary's capability for payments
jokes.punchline: Bizarre double metaphor that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly illogical metaphor - subverts expectation of 'boy trapped in man's body' structure

jokes.text: Do you want me to take off my shirt so that you can see all the knife handles sticking out of my back?
jokes.setup: Bill claiming innocence after being blamed
jokes.punchline: Offers to show metaphorical knife wounds
jokes.explanation: Good Bill melodrama - visualization of betrayal metaphor is effectively ridiculous

jokes.text: It's like I'm on the sacrificial altar and suddenly everyone is wearing hoods.
jokes.setup: Bill feeling targeted by committee
jokes.punchline: Cult sacrifice metaphor for Congressional hearing
jokes.explanation: Bill's paranoid metaphor escalation - committee becomes ritual sacrifice cult

jokes.text: But Jonah Ryan is strong. And if Jonah Ryan can help one man who is dealing with workplace sexual harassment, then this entire ordeal will have been worth it.
jokes.setup: Jonah's closing statement about strength
jokes.punchline: Turns personal victimization into heroic mission
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah grandiosity - making himself a martyr for workplace harassment victims

jokes.text: You know, there are probably fairer trials happening right now in the back of parking lots where the defendant is wearing a mailbag on his head.
jokes.setup: Bill's final complaint about the hearing process
jokes.punchline: Compares Congressional hearing to vigilante justice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Bill hyperbole - parking lot kangaroo court imagery is wonderfully vivid and absurd

jokes.text: Sorry, I was practicing in the mirror.
jokes.setup: Asked to raise right hand, Bill raises wrong hand
jokes.punchline: Admits to rehearsing basic oath procedure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bill overthinking - even practices raising his hand wrong because of mirror reversal

tmdb_overview: While Selina fields questions from the press, members of her staff testify before a congressional committee about whether the Families First vote was intentionally sabotaged – and who was really behind the data breach.
insight: Veep's cringe apex: 75 jokes in 34 minutes, dominated by character-driven absurdist chaos.
